title: Isoloma elegans
intro: Isoloma elegans, also known as Isoloma, is a plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly referred to as broomrapes or Indian paintbrushes. Traditionally, Isoloma elegans has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. Isoloma elegans is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Isoloma elegans is a species of plant in the family Cephalotaceae. It belongs to the genus Isoloma, which is a group of liverworts. The classification of Isoloma elegans is further specified as Isoloma elegans (Hook.) Trevis. The family Cephalotaceae is a group of liverwort species, and Isoloma elegans is one of the species within this family. The exact classification of Isoloma elegans can be broken down into its various taxonomic ranks.

preparations: Isoloma elegans has been used in traditional herbal medicine in New Zealand, particularly by the Maori people, for its potential health benefits.
